title: CRISIS PSYCHIATRIC CARE FOR YOUTH SUBACUTE - 211info
text: ALBERTINA KERR CRISIS PSYCHIATRIC CARE FOR YOUTH CRISIS PSYCHIATRIC CARE FOR YOUTH SUBACUTE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides safe and compassionate care to support youth stabilization, with most discharged within three weeks. The program offers a nurturing environment where youth can recover, reflect, and build skills for well-being and growth. Services include psychiatric evaluation and medication management, individual and family therapy, skill-building groups, daily therapeutic arts and recreation, continued school enrollment through Multnomah Education Service District, access to primary care assessments by nurse practitioners, and rapid psychological testing. 832 NE 162nd Avenue Portland, OR 97230 Eligibility: Ages 5-17 Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 408-4705 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers (503) 408-4705 Main phone Intake (503) 408-4705 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for information. Accepts referrals from emergency departments, hospitals, outpatient clinicians, acute psychiatric units, and crisis response teams. FEES: Accepts OHP and some private insurance DOCUMENTS: Insurance information, if applicable LANGUAGES: English, interpretation by arrangement AREA SERVED: State of Oregon SITE HOURS: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Services: Crisis Residential Treatment * Disorders of Infancy/Childhood If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
